smckit
  • API Reference
  • Extensions: State-Space Models
  • View page source

Extensions: State-Space Models

The state-space model framework provides a formal HMM lens on SMC methods, including a differentiable PSMC via JAX. See SSM Framework for the conceptual overview.

class smckit.ext.ssm.SmcStateSpace[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract state-space model for SMC methods.

Subclasses define the coalescent-to-HMM mapping by implementing three methods: transition_matrix(), emission_matrix(), and initial_distribution(). The base class provides generic log_likelihood() and fit() using these building blocks.

n_states

Number of hidden states (coalescent time intervals).

Type:

int

n_obs

Number of observation symbols.

Type:

int

abstractmethod transition_matrix(params)[source]

Compute the (n_states, n_states) transition matrix.

Parameters:

params (np.ndarray) – Flat parameter vector.

Returns:

Row-stochastic transition matrix.

Return type:

np.ndarray

abstractmethod emission_matrix(params)[source]

Compute the (n_obs, n_states) emission matrix.

Parameters:

params (np.ndarray) – Flat parameter vector.

Returns:

Emission probabilities per symbol per state.

Return type:

np.ndarray

abstractmethod initial_distribution(params)[source]

Compute the (n_states,) initial state distribution.

Parameters:

params (np.ndarray) – Flat parameter vector.

Returns:

Probability vector summing to 1.

Return type:

np.ndarray

log_likelihood(params, observations)[source]

Compute log P(observations | params) via the scaled forward algorithm.

Parameters:

  • params (np.ndarray) – Flat parameter vector.

  • observations (list of np.ndarray) – Each element is an int8 observation sequence (e.g. one chromosome).

Returns:

Total log-likelihood summed over all sequences.

Return type:

float

fit(observations, params_init, method='gradient', n_iterations=None, **kwargs)[source]

Fit the model to observations.

Parameters:

  • observations (list of np.ndarray) – Observation sequences (one per chromosome/record).

  • params_init (np.ndarray) – Initial parameter vector.

  • method (str) – "gradient" for JAX gradient-based optimization (requires JAX), "em" for EM with Hooke-Jeeves M-step (NumPy/Numba fallback).

  • n_iterations (int, optional) – Number of iterations. Defaults to 500 for gradient, 30 for EM.

  • **kwargs – Passed to the backend fitting function.

Returns:

Optimization results.

Return type:

FitResult

class smckit.ext.ssm.PsmcSSM(pattern='4+5*3+4', alpha=0.1, divergence=False)[source]

Bases: SmcStateSpace

PSMC formulated as a state-space model.

Parameters:

  • pattern (str) – PSMC pattern string (e.g. "4+5*3+4").

  • alpha (float) – Time-interval spacing parameter.

  • divergence (bool) – Whether to include a divergence-time parameter.

transition_matrix(params)[source]

Compute the (n_states, n_states) transition matrix.

Parameters:

params (np.ndarray) – Flat parameter vector.

Returns:

Row-stochastic transition matrix.

Return type:

np.ndarray

emission_matrix(params)[source]

Compute the (n_obs, n_states) emission matrix.

Parameters:

params (np.ndarray) – Flat parameter vector.

Returns:

Emission probabilities per symbol per state.

Return type:

np.ndarray

initial_distribution(params)[source]

Compute the (n_states,) initial state distribution.

Parameters:

params (np.ndarray) – Flat parameter vector.

Returns:

Probability vector summing to 1.

Return type:

np.ndarray

make_initial_params(sum_L, sum_n, max_t=15.0, tr_ratio=4.0, random_init=0.01, seed=None)[source]

Create an initial parameter vector from data statistics.

Parameters:

  • sum_L (int) – Total number of sites (excluding missing).

  • sum_n (int) – Total number of heterozygous sites.

  • max_t (float) – Maximum coalescent time (units of 2N0).

  • tr_ratio (float) – Initial theta/rho ratio.

  • random_init (float) – Amplitude of random perturbation for lambda values.

  • seed (int, optional) – Random seed for reproducibility.

Returns:

Parameter vector [theta, rho, max_t, lam_0, …, lam_{n_free-1}].

Return type:

np.ndarray

to_physical_units(params, mu=1.25e-08, generation_time=25.0, window_size=100)[source]

Convert fitted parameters to physical units.

Parameters:

  • params (np.ndarray) – Fitted parameter vector.

  • mu (float) – Per-base per-generation mutation rate.

  • generation_time (float) – Generation time in years.

  • window_size (int) – Window size used in input data.

Returns:

Keys: time_years, ne, lambda_k, n0, theta, rho.

Return type:

dict

class smckit.ext.ssm.FitResult(params, log_likelihood, n_iterations, converged, history=<factory>)[source]

Result from fitting an SMC state-space model.

Parameters:

  • params (np.ndarray) – Optimized parameter vector [theta, rho, max_t, lam_0, …, lam_{n_free-1}].

  • log_likelihood (float) – Final log-likelihood value.

  • n_iterations (int) – Number of iterations performed.

  • converged (bool) – Whether the optimizer converged.

  • history (list of dict) – Per-iteration records (loss, params, etc.).

params: ndarray
log_likelihood: float
n_iterations: int
converged: bool
history: list[dict]
